recent_coach_change: No recent change; Argentine Nestor Lorenzo has led Colombia since June 2022 (succeeding Reinaldo Rueda) and oversaw qualification.
qualification_path: Finished a strong third (some sources second) in CONMEBOL qualifying with around 28 points from 18 matches, securing automatic qualification behind Argentina. Second-best defensive record in CONMEBOL.
key_players: Luis Diaz (Liverpool) — talisman winger, scored in the 4-0 friendly rout of Mexico
James Rodriguez (Minnesota United / Club Leon) — captain, 34, creative leader but short on minutes and form
Jhon Arias / Juan Camilo 'Cucho' Hernandez (Real Betis) — in-form forward, among La Liga's top scorers
Juan Fernando Quintero (River Plate) — attacking midfielder, likely his last World Cup

injuries_doubts.issue: Fitness/match-sharpness concern after fewer than 200 minutes of recent club football; on an individual training program

squad_notes: Lorenzo named his 26-man squad on 25 May 2026. James Rodriguez retained as captain despite limited minutes, with the staff running a special physical program for him. Notable omissions include Wilmar Barrios (Zenit) and Rafael Santos Borre. Real Betis forward 'Cucho' Hernandez among the in-form inclusions.
warmup_assessment: Strong recent form: thrashed Mexico 4-0 and beat Australia 3-0 in late-2025 friendlies, showing attacking sharpness and defensive solidity ahead of the tournament.
shape_verdict: Colombia look in good shape as group favourites alongside Portugal, blending Luis Diaz's quality with a solid defence; the main question mark is the fitness and form of veteran captain James Rodriguez.

news.headline: Lorenzo names 26-man Colombia World Cup squad led by James Rodriguez and Luis Diaz
Concerns over James Rodriguez's lack of minutes; Lorenzo reveals special fitness plan
